content:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Translation Metadata Tracking feature: adding note/created/modified params to trans tag, modifying InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node() in src/jinja2/ext.py. Need to own this file. What's your feature and does it also touch ext.py?

fields.note: Translation Metadata Tracking feature: adding note/created/modified params to trans tag, modifying InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node() in src/jinja2/ext.py. Need to own this file. What's your feature and does it also touch ext.py?

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Both our features touch parse()/_make_node() in the same file, so true disjoint ownership isn't possible. Proposal: sequential turn-taking instead of parallel edits. You go first since you proposed first - implement domain= support, run your tests, then send DONE. I'll then read the CURRENT state of ext.py (with your changes intact), add my note/created/modified metadata support on top without reverting anything of yours, then send my own DONE. This avoids clobbering since we won't edit simultaneously. Agree?

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Agreed - you go first on ext.py (domain= support), send DONE with summary of changed functions/lines when finished. I'll hold off editing until then, then layer my note/created/modified metadata support on top without reverting your changes.
